lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
Epiphanius Bishop of Cyprus convenes a Synod to condemn the Books of Origen.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Epiphanius Bishop of Cyprus convenes a Synod to condemn the Books of Origen.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He moreover renewed his friendship with Epiphanius bishop of Constantia in Cyprus, with whom he had formerly been at variance.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: For Theophilus accused Epiphanius of entertaining low thoughts of God, by supposing him to have a human form.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now although Theophilus was really unchanged in sentiment, and had denounced those who thought that the divinity was human in form, yet on account of his hatred of others, he openly denied his own convictions; and he now professed to be friendly with Epiphanius, as if he had altered his mind and agreed with him in his views of God.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He then managed it so that Epiphanius by letter should convene a Synod of the bishops in Cyprus, in order to condemn the writings of Origen.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Epiphanius being on account of his extraordinary piety a man of simple mind and manners was easily influenced by the letters of Theophilus: having therefore assembled a council of bishops in that island, he caused a prohibition to be therein made of the reading of Origen’s works.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He also wrote to John, exhorting him to abstain from the study of Origen’s books, and to convoke a Synod for decreeing the same thing as he had done.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Accordingly when Theophilus had in this way deluded Epiphanius, who was famous for his piety, seeing his design prosper according to his wish, he became more confident, and himself also assembled a great number of bishops.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: In that convention, pursuing the same course as Epiphanius, he caused a like sentence of condemnation to be pronounced on the writings of Origen, who had been dead nearly two hundred years: not having this as his first object, but rather his purpose of revenge on Dioscorus and his brethren.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: John paying but little attention to the communications of Epiphanius and Theophilus, was intent on instructing the churches; and he flourished more and more as a preacher, but made no account of the plots which were laid against him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: As soon, however, as it became apparent to every body that Theophilus was endeavoring to divest John of his bishopric, then all those who had any ill-will against John, combined in calumniating him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And thus many of the clergy, and many of those in office, and of those who had great influence at the court, believing that they had found an opportunity now of avenging themselves upon John, exerted themselves to procure the convocation of a Grand Synod at Constantinople, partly by sending letters and partly by dispatching messengers in all directions for that purpose.
